title: Advancing Omnimodal Embodied Agents from Isolated Skills to Everyday Physical Autonomy
abstract: arXiv:2606.27251v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Building persistent embodied agents in unstructured environments demands unified orchestration of heterogeneous tools spanning both cyber (APIs, IoT) and physical (manipulation, navigation) domains, coupled with autonomous recovery from physical failures that inevitably arise over extended operation. Existing systems treat these as separate problems: VLM-based planners lack a unified cyber-physical action space, agent frameworks accumulate unbounded context that degrades temporal coherence, and VLA policies execute open-loop without detecting their own failures. We argue that persistent autonomy requires not a monolithic model but a hierarchical asynchronous architecture with explicit separation of planning, memory, and verification. To this end, we present OmniAct, a framework integrating a multimodal semantic planner for skill routing across unified action spaces, an adaptive hierarchical memory with event-boundary-driven compression

Title: Advancing Omnimodal Embodied Agents from Isolated Skills to Everyday Physical Autonomy
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27251v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Building persistent embodied agents in unstructured environments demands unified orchestration of heterogeneous tools spanning both cyber (APIs, IoT) and physical (manipulation, navigation) domains, coupled with autonomous recovery from physical failures that inevitably arise over extended operation. Existing systems treat these as separate problems: VLM-based planners lack a unified cyber-physical action space, agent frameworks accumulate unbounded context that degrades temporal coherence, and VLA policies execute open-loop without detecting their own failures. We argue that persistent autonomy requires not a monolithic model but a hierarchical asynchronous architecture with explicit separation of planning, memory, and verification. To this end, we present OmniAct, a framework integrating a multimodal semantic planner for skill routing across unified action spaces, an adaptive hierarchical memory with event-boundary-driven compression
translated_title: 推动全能具身智能体从孤立技能迈向日常物理自主
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.27251v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：在非结构化环境中构建持久性具身智能体，需要统一协调涵盖网络（API、物联网）和物理（操作、导航）领域的异构工具，同时具备对长时间运行中不可避免的物理故障的自主恢复能力。现有系统将这些视为分离问题：基于视觉语言模型的规划器缺乏统一的网络-物理动作空间，智能体框架积累无界上下文导致时间连贯性下降，而视觉-语言-动作策略在未检测自身故障的情况下执行开环操作。我们认为，持久自主性需要的不是单一模型，而是一种分层异步架构，明确分离规划、记忆和验证功能。为此，我们提出OmniAct框架，该框架集成多模态语义规划器以实现跨统一动作空间的技能路由，并配备具有事件边界驱动压缩的自适应分层记忆。
tldr: 本文提出OmniAct框架，通过分层异步架构统一网络与物理动作空间，并引入自适应分层记忆机制，解决了现有具身智能体在持久自主操作中缺乏统一动作空间、上下文无限累积和无法自主恢复故障的核心问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes OmniAct, a hierarchical asynchronous framework that unifies cyber-physical action spaces with adaptive hierarchical memory, addressing the core challenges of persistent embodied autonomy including lack of unified action spaces, unbounded context accumulation, and inability to autonomously recover from failures.
